mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.25% represents a restrictive monetary stance that suppresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates. Sustained high rates above 5.0% produce negative S&P 500 forward returns as the cost of capital rises and future cash flows are discounted more heavily.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS reflects consumer spending momentum. Declining retail sales signal weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 366166.0 million dollars as of September 2006
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or correlation between RSAFS growth and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 4.6% reflects tight labor market conditions. Rising unemployment above 4.8% signals deteriorating labor demand and recessionary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations decline over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.8%, or Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns exceeds -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization. Sustained INDPRO growth above 0.3% month-over-month signals robust supply-side expansion and pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as earnings surprises materialize.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or correlation is not significantly positive (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST and retail sales RSAFS jointly capture consumer and construction demand. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously, aggregate demand weakness produces negative 20-day SPX returns. The interaction effect is stronger than either series alone because it signals broad-based demand contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1665.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 366166.0 million dollars as of August-September 2006
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand weakness
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS momentum) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window, or the joint signal underperforms single-series signals by > 20% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment PAYEMS and unemployment rate UNRATE jointly measure labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 0.1% month-over-month while UNRATE rises, the combination signals imminent labor market deterioration and recession risk, producing negative 60-day SPX returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp from prior month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135613.0 thousand, UNRATE at 4.6% as of September 2006
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression against forward return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 203.7 combined with restrictive FEDFUNDS at 5.25% indicates the Fed is actively fighting inflation. When CPI growth exceeds 2.5% annualized and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, the real interest rate is elevated, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative 20-day returns as monetary policy remains restrictive.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.7, FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of August-September 2006
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond FEDFUNDS alone (R² improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth GDPC1 represents the broadest measure of aggregate demand. When quarterly GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% annualized, it signals economic deceleration and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11388.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2006
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of quarters when GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).
